## II. How to Read the Map

::: {.aside .meta-note}
**META:** Names the machinery only. The thesis is in the intro; the how-to-use-it diagnostic is in the conclusion. This section just says what the three kinds of part are, so §III can use them. No "spine," no "carving joints." Emphasis stays sparing.
:::

The map has three kinds of part, and everything after depends on telling them apart.

The axes are the framing choices, set before any evidence comes in. You met two already: what counts as doom, from extinction to loss of control to present harm, and over what horizon. The third is how much a model's current behavior should count against a structural prior. These are the labels on the [interactive tool](https://lifeuniversesafety.com/doom-assumptions/index.html), and two people who set them differently can trade p(doom) figures all day and never once contradict each other.

The tree is the mechanism, carried over from the first two posts: does AI raise the danger at all, through one dominant system or many, does that system know what it is doing, does it expect the catastrophe it causes. The gallery walks the tree, one worldview to a branch.

The cruxes are the factual bets, the points where evidence has a say and people actually move. They read more clearly once the positions are on the table, so they come after the gallery.
